description: Target-token denylist for check_core_purity. The purity guard reads its token set FROM THIS FILE (it is not hardcoded in the script). In the generic, public factory_core this token set is EMPTY on purpose: a generic core names nothing target-specific, so there is nothing to catch. A consuming target fills in its OWN tokens here as part of its private configuration; the target's filled-in denylist is the TARGET's config and is not shipped in the public core.

shape.tokens: An array of lowercase, whole-word target-specific tokens that have no business appearing anywhere in a generic core (identifiers or string literals). Each token is a single alphanumeric run. Matching is COMPONENT EQUALITY, not substring: text is lowercased and split on every non-alphanumeric character into runs (snake_case, dotted, and punctuated names all split), and a token matches only if it EQUALS one of those runs. So a token 'acme' would trip 'ACME_KEY' (runs {acme, key}) and 'acme_county' (runs {acme, county}) but NEVER 'acmestone' or a longer word that merely contains it. Choose tokens that are distinctive to your target (a company codename, a jurisdiction, a vendor, a regulatory acronym) and unlikely to collide with ordinary English or common code identifiers. The guard scans factory_core/**/*.py via AST (comments are exempt); the sibling schema/module/fixture token tests apply the same list to non-.py data files.

instructions: To configure this for YOUR target: replace the empty 'tokens' array below with your target-specific tokens, lowercase and as single alphanumeric runs (e.g. a company codename, jurisdiction, vendor name, or regulatory acronym).
Keep the list distinctive and collision-free: prefer names that will never appear as ordinary English words or common code identifiers, since matching is whole-run equality.
Do NOT ship your filled-in denylist in the public core. It is your target's OWN config. The public factory_core ships this file with an EMPTY token set (the generic core has nothing to catch).
The 'examples' field below is illustrative only and is NEVER scanned by the guard. Delete or ignore it; do not treat its contents as an active denylist.
After editing, run `make check-purity` (or `make ship`) to confirm the core is clean against your configured tokens.

examples.note: ILLUSTRATIVE ONLY — replace with your own. These are fictional placeholders (a made-up company, product, and vendor), NOT any real target's terms. They are stored under 'examples' precisely so the guard does NOT read them as active tokens.
